1.copy:
The DCX Trifecta: Why Three Vendors Is One Too Many

Most enterprise marketing programmes are built on a contradiction.

Marketing says the data shows X. Analytics says it shows Y. The technology team is still waiting on an API from three months ago.

When marketing, analytics, and technology sit in three separate vendor contracts, the data never quite lines up — and the customer experience shows it.

We see this constantly in financial services. A bank with a world-class CRM, a separate analytics platform, and a third agency running campaigns. Three teams. Three datasets. Three versions of the customer.

The result: personalisation that feels generic. Journeys that don't connect. Attribution that no one fully trusts.

The DCX Trifecta — Marketing + Analytics + Technology as a single integrated capability — is the answer to this. Not three separate engagements. One programme.

We built this for a leading US financial services firm. 36 million email sends, automated, unified, measurably tied to retention outcomes.

What does your current martech architecture look like — unified team or separate vendors for each pillar?

#DCX #DigitalMarketing #BFSI #Salesforce #CustomerExperience
1.firstComment: Full piece on the blog — [link to sumerusolutions.com]

caseStudy.label: Case study
caseStudy.text: A leading US financial services firm we worked with — 36M email sends, automated, unified, built in from day one. Three vendors became one integrated programme.

2.copy:
In January 2026, the FDA deployed its own agentic AI system agency-wide.

That's worth sitting with.

The regulator responsible for evaluating clinical AI now uses agentic AI internally. Which means when they examine your AI governance framework, they know exactly what a well-governed system looks like — and what it doesn't.

FDA Total Product Lifecycle (TPLC) requires continuous monitoring, model drift tracking, and Predetermined Change Control Plans for every AI-enabled clinical system.

1,250+ FDA-cleared AI-enabled devices as of July 2025. Approximately 100 new approvals per year.

Most health systems are deploying AI copilots and agents across clinical workflows. Very few have:

→ An immutable audit trail for every AI interaction
→ A guardrail pipeline that prevents inappropriate outputs
→ A provable chain of custody for model decisions

'The model decided it' is not documentation. It's an audit finding.

Orchestra AI's Cockpit is built to close this gap — immutable logs, guardrail enforcement, full observability across every AI tool in your environment.

If a regulator asked for every AI interaction your clinical systems made in the last 90 days, could you produce it — or would that be a scramble?

#AIGovernance #HealthcareAI #FDATPLC #OrchestraAI #HIPAA
2.firstComment: We can help build the audit infrastructure. copilot@sumerusolutions.com

3.copy:
27% encryption maturity.

That's where utilities sit — against a 39% global average.

It's the largest encryption readiness gap of any critical infrastructure sector. And NERC CIP-012-2, which mandates protection of real-time operational data in transit between control centres, goes live in July 2026.

But the more dangerous exposure isn't data in transit.

It's firmware signing.

Legacy OT devices — GE Mark VI turbine controllers, ABB inverters, Schweitzer protection relays — have operational lifespans of 20+ years and no firmware update path.

These devices authenticate firmware updates using digital signatures. Under a 'Sign Today, Forge Tomorrow' threat model, adversaries collect today's signed commands and firmware, then wait until quantum computing allows them to forge equivalent signatures.

The result: malicious firmware loaded to PLCs. Grid safety equipment impersonated by an attacker's workstation.

CISA published OT-specific PQC guidance in January 2026. Federal buying guidance explicitly covers critical infrastructure operators.

Gateway-based PQC is the architectural answer for devices with no firmware path. But it requires knowing what you have first — and most utilities don't.

Where does your OT environment sit on encryption maturity — and is NERC CIP-012-2 already on your July roadmap?

#QuantumSecurity #NERCCIP #OTSecurity #PQC #CriticalInfrastructure
3.firstComment: Start with a cryptographic discovery. copilot@sumerusolutions.com

slides.label: Scene 1 (Cover / Hook)
slides.headline: What happens when nearly every person on your team meditates
slides.body: Open on a quiet moment — an office, a pause between calls, someone's hands on a keyboard. VO: 'We don't talk about this enough at Sumeru.'

slides.label: Scene 2 (The Fact)
slides.headline: Nearly 100% of Sumeru's team are active meditators
slides.body: This isn't a corporate wellness initiative. It's part of who we are. The company was built on a belief: technology created by human consciousness, in service of human excellence.
slides.scene: Cut to a team member speaking naturally. 15-20 seconds.

slides.label: Scene 3 (What It Changes #1)
slides.headline: Calmer under pressure
slides.body: Enterprise technology delivery is high-stakes. Deadlines, stakeholders, competing priorities. A regulated mind makes better decisions in a pressured room than a reactive one. Our clients notice this.
slides.scene: Second team member. Different background. Authentic.

slides.label: Scene 4 (What It Changes #2)
slides.headline: Better listening in discovery
slides.body: Most consulting engagements fail in the listening phase — not the delivery phase. When you're not rehearsing your next point, you hear what's actually being said. That changes the quality of the solution.
slides.scene: Third team member or return to first.

slides.label: Scene 5 (What It Changes #3)
slides.headline: Faster problem-solving
slides.body: Clarity of mind isn't a soft skill. It's a performance advantage. When the noise is lower, the signal is cleaner. Teams that can quiet themselves think more precisely under pressure.
slides.scene: Wide shot of team working, natural energy.

slides.label: Scene 6 (The Foundation)
slides.headline: Innovation inspired by human excellence
slides.body: That's not a tagline. It's the operating principle. Sumeru was founded on the idea that the best technology comes from the most developed human consciousness.
slides.scene: Founder or senior leader. Direct, genuine.

slides.headline: Does your team have a shared practice?
slides.body: We'd genuinely like to know. Drop it in the comments — we'll share more about what this looks like at Sumeru in a follow-up post.
slides.scene: Warm close, team together. End card: Sumeru logo.

slides.label: Slide 1 (Cover)
slides.headline: Why patients leave before they even arrive
slides.subtitle: The digital front door problem — and what a fixed one looks like.

slides.label: Slide 2 (Context)
slides.body: Health systems have invested millions in EHR platforms and patient portals. Most patients still call to book an appointment. The portal isn't broken. The experience around it is.

slides.label: Slide 3 (Where it fractures #1)
slides.headline: The search-to-schedule gap
slides.body: A patient searches for a specialist. They find your website. They can't tell which location is right for them. They click away. → This happens before they ever touch your portal.

slides.label: Slide 4 (Where it fractures #2)
slides.headline: The login wall
slides.body: New patient, first visit. They hit a portal registration form with 9 fields and a CAPTCHA. → This is not a patient experience. It's an IT implementation.

slides.label: Slide 5 (Where it fractures #3)
slides.headline: The channel dead end
slides.body: Patient has a question after their appointment. They try the portal. It says 'message your care team.' 3 days later: no reply. They call. → Avoidable contact. Every time.

slides.label: Slide 6 (What fixed looks like)
slides.headline: One connected journey
slides.body: Search → schedule → intake → care → follow-up. Each step connected. No channel switches. No repeated information. → Salesforce Health Cloud makes this the operating layer.

slides.label: Slide 7 (The result)
slides.headline: What health systems measure
slides.body:
→ Portal adoption rate up
→ Avoidable contact centre calls down
→ Patient satisfaction scores up
→ Clinical staff time freed from admin

slides.label: Slide 8 (CTA)
slides.headline: Save this if you're working on patient experience
slides.subtitle: What's the single biggest drop-off point in your patient digital journey right now? Drop it in the comments — we read every one.

slides.label: Slide 1 (Cover)
slides.headline: 5 things financial services gets wrong about ISO 42001
slides.subtitle: Most banks think they're closer to compliance than they are.

slides.label: Slide 2 (Context)
slides.body: 83% of Fortune 500 procurement teams will require ISO 42001 alignment from vendors by 2027. EU AI Act high-risk obligations for credit and fraud AI hit full enforcement in August 2026. The certification clock is running.

slides.label: Slide 3 (Misconception 1)
slides.headline: 'It's just a policy document'
slides.body: ISO 42001 requires a live AI Management System — documented risk processes, continuous monitoring, audit evidence. A policy is not a system. → Regulators and procurement teams will know the difference.

slides.label: Slide 4 (Misconception 2)
slides.headline: 'We're already compliant via SR 11-7'
slides.body: SR 11-7 covers model risk management. ISO 42001 covers the governance system around AI systems. They're complementary. Having one doesn't mean you have the other.

slides.label: Slide 5 (Misconception 3)
slides.headline: 'Starting from scratch takes too long'
slides.body: If your organisation holds ISO 27001, you're 30–40% of the way to ISO 42001 already. Shared Annex SL management structure means your system, your risk methodology, your audit process — all carry over.

slides.label: Slide 6 (Misconception 4)
slides.headline: 'EU AI Act doesn't apply to us'
slides.body: If you run credit decisioning, fraud detection, or customer-facing AI models — you're in scope. Full enforcement: August 2026. That's not a future risk. It's a current compliance obligation.

slides.label: Slide 7 (Misconception 5)
slides.headline: 'We'll handle it when procurement asks'
slides.body: AI liability insurance carriers are offering 15–25% premium discounts for ISO 42001-certified organisations. The cost of waiting is already showing up on the balance sheet.

slides.label: Slide 8 (CTA)
slides.headline: Save this for your next governance review
slides.subtitle: Which of these misconceptions have you run into in your own ISO 42001 programme? Comments open.

caseStudy.label: Case study
caseStudy.text: A leading European universal bank we worked with completed ISO 42001 gap assessment in 6 weeks using their existing ISO 27001 programme. Certification-ready in 4 months.

7.copy:
Healthcare has the longest data sensitivity window of any regulated sector.

And most PQC planning hasn't caught up with that fact.

HIPAA requires patient data retention for up to 50 years. Genomic data carries no expiry date at all.

That means the encrypted records a health system creates today — for a patient born in 2000 — need to remain secure against adversarial capabilities that don't yet exist at scale.

'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' (HNDL) is not a theoretical risk in healthcare. It's an active one. Adversaries are collecting encrypted patient data today, with the explicit intent to decrypt it once quantum computing reaches sufficient capability.

FDA Section 524B adds a second pressure layer: devices submitted without forward-looking cryptographic planning may be refused for market. And with IoMT device lifecycles running 10+ years, crypto-agility has to be designed in from the start — not retrofitted later.

We're currently working with a major medical device company on a PQC readiness engagement. What cryptographic discovery surfaces in a healthcare environment is consistently more complex than security teams expect.

Has your organisation factored genomic data retention into its quantum risk assessment — or is that still a future conversation?

#QuantumSecurity #PQC #HIPAA #HealthcareSecurity #HNDL
7.firstComment: We're working with a leading global medical device company on exactly this. copilot@sumerusolutions.com

8.copy:
A field guide to "We've already tried everything."

Five translations every consultant knows:

1. "We tried one vendor, once, in 2021."
Tried one approach, briefly. The problem was declared unsolvable. The vendor moved on.

2. "We tried the right thing at the wrong time."
The org wasn't ready. The budget got cut. The champion left. The idea wasn't wrong — the conditions were.

3. "We tried it without cross-functional buy-in."
IT owned it. Marketing didn't know about it. The data sat in a system no one queried.
This one's the most common.

4. "We tried it in a pilot that never scaled."
Proof of concept: successful. Enterprise rollout: stalled at governance, procurement, and change management.

5. "We actually did try everything. We need a fresh perspective."
This one's rare. And when it's genuine, it's the most interesting conversation we have.

Which translation do you hear most?
We'll go first — #3 is the one we walk into most often.

#Consulting #DigitalTransformation #EnterpriseIT #Sumeru

9.copy:
Most utility customers say they'd prefer to handle billing disputes and outage questions online.

Most utility portals technically let them.

So why does contact centre volume stay stubbornly high?

The answer usually isn't the portal. It's the experience around it.

Three patterns we see most often:

→ The portal exists, but customers don't know it can solve their specific problem. Nobody told them.

→ The portal solves simple queries. The moment a customer has a nuanced billing question or a multi-day outage, the portal routes them back to the phone.

→ The portal experience is functional, but the proactive communications are missing. Customers call because they haven't heard anything — not because they can't self-serve.

The cost-to-serve difference between a digital interaction and an agent-handled call is 10–15x.

That's not a small efficiency opportunity. For a mid-size utility handling 2M contacts per year, moving 20% to digital is a meaningful P&L impact — before you account for customer satisfaction improvements.

Salesforce Energy & Utilities Cloud gives utilities the customer data layer to build genuinely useful self-service. The DCX work is designing the experience that makes customers actually use it.

What's driving the most avoidable call volume in your contact centre right now — billing, outage updates, or something else?

#DCX #UtilityCustomerExperience #Salesforce #CustomerService #DigitalTransformation

slides.headline: NERC auditors are asking for AI decision logs
slides.body: VO over graphic of a utility control room: 'NERC CIP-015-1 mandates Internal Network Security Monitoring by October 2028. AI is the only tool that processes alert volume at that scale.'
slides.scene: Opening graphic or B-roll. Establishes the regulatory context immediately.

slides.label: Scene 2 (The Problem)
slides.headline: But there's a gap between deploying AI and governing it
slides.body: Most utilities deploying AI for OT security monitoring have one problem: when a NERC auditor asks what the AI decided and why — they can't answer. 'The model flagged it' is not documentation.
slides.scene: Talking head — practitioner tone. Direct, specific.

slides.label: Scene 3 (Why it matters)
slides.headline: NERC auditors have been explicit
slides.body: Contemporaneous audit documentation is required for every AI determination in a NERC-monitored environment. Not a summary. Not a log export from last quarter. Contemporaneous. Every. Determination.
slides.scene: Pull quote on screen: 'The model decided it is an audit finding, not documentation.'

slides.label: Scene 4 (The scale of the gap)
slides.headline: 9% of energy organisations conduct AI red-teaming
slides.body: That's from CrowdStrike's 2026 report. 89% year-over-year increase in AI-assisted attacks on critical infrastructure. The threat surface is expanding. The governance infrastructure isn't keeping pace.
slides.scene: Simple stat graphic. Let the numbers land.

slides.label: Scene 5 (The solution)
slides.headline: Orchestra AI Cockpit — built for exactly this
slides.body: Immutable audit trails. Every AI interaction, every guardrail trigger, full chain of custody — logged in real time. Not reconstructed after the fact. The technical evidence a NERC auditor actually needs.
slides.scene: Screen recording or product graphic of Cockpit dashboard. Keep it clean.

slides.label: Scene 6 (CTA)
slides.headline: If a NERC auditor walked in today…
slides.body: …and asked for the audit trail on your AI-assisted security decisions — what would you hand them? That's the question we help utilities answer. Link in comments.
slides.scene: Return to talking head. Warm close.
